title: cocculus palmatus
intro:
Cocculus palmatus, also known as the Indian berry or arracacha, is a tropical plant native to Southeast Asia and Australia, its fruit and seeds containing a toxic alkaloid called cocculus.

The medicinal properties of Cocculus palmatus reside in its seeds, which have been used in traditional medicine for their purgative and emetic effects. These seeds are known to induce vomiting and stimulate bowel movements, making them useful in treating conditions such as constipation and indigestion.

The health benefits of Cocculus palmatus include its ability to improve digestion, reduce inflammation, and even exhibit antimicrobial properties. It has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.

The herbal preparations of Cocculus palmatus typically involve drying and processing the seeds, which can be used in various forms such as decoctions, infusions, and powders, often in combination with other herbs to enhance their medicinal properties.
